PULMONOLOGY CONSULTATION
Provider: R. Patel, MD — Pulmonary and Critical Care
Encounter date: 2025-09-05

HISTORY
Mr. Doe presents for outpatient pulmonology follow-up after a recent
hospitalization for right upper lobe pneumonia. Discharge imaging showed
residual right upper lobe density not fully explained by infection. He
notes ongoing dry cough and a 12-pound weight loss over six months. No
hemoptysis. He is a former smoker (45 pack-years), quit 2014.

EXAMINATION
Lungs with diminished breath sounds at the right apex. No wheezing. SpO2
96% on room air. Heart sounds regular. No clubbing.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN
Right upper lobe density, concerning for malignancy in this patient with
significant tobacco history and unintentional weight loss. Will obtain
dedicated CT chest with contrast. If suspicious lesion confirmed, will
proceed to CT-guided core biopsy and refer to thoracic oncology. Given
clinical suspicion, the working differential includes primary lung
carcinoma; however, no diagnosis of lung cancer has been established at
this visit and we explicitly defer that determination to tissue.

Patient and family counseled on the differential and the recommended
workup. He is agreeable to proceed.

R. Patel, MD — electronically signed 2025-09-05 10:22
